steps.title: Follow the time shift
steps.content: The first sentence describes what scientists thought in 1929. The next sentence says the plant discovery would not come until decades later, so the transition must signal a later development.

steps.title: Remove the wrong signals
steps.content:
• Choice A signals restatement, but the plant discovery is not a restatement of the animal-only assumption.
  • Choice B signals an example, not a later contradiction.
  • Choice D points backward in time, while the discovery came later.

steps.title: Mark the later discovery
steps.content: Eventually signals movement to a later point in time and matches the phrase decades later. The answer is C.
